title: No One Has Secretly Broken RSA-2048 or RSA-4096 — Here's the Science
why:
- The largest quantum factoring demonstration via Shor’s algorithm has successfully factored the number 21 (five bits), while RSA-4096 involves factoring a 4,096-bit number—a gap of a trillion trillion times in difficulty.
- Factoring RSA-2048 using a quantum computer would require about 100,000 to 1,000,000 physical qubits at error rates a billion times lower than current technology allows.
- As of early 2026, the best quantum computing hardware features around 1,000 noisy qubits, with only initial progress in error correction.
- Every previous public claim of breaking RSA-2048 has been debunked, withdrawn, or shown to be misleading—there has been a 100% failure rate for such claims.
- No scientific, supply chain, talent, or energy indicators exist to support the possibility of a secret breakthrough in factoring RSA-2048 or RSA-4096.
- Major governments are continuing with gradual, multi-year post-quantum cryptography transitions, showing no indication that RSA has been secretly broken.
